meta.title: InfoBIM – A modular runtime environment for Engineering Automation
meta.description: InfoBIM IFC is a modular runtime for Engineering Automation that exposes reusable Capabilities so humans and AI agents can execute reliable BIM logic directly from IFC models.
hero.tagline: The interface between BIM data, Human Engineers, and AI Agents
hero.title: InfoBIM: The Capability Engine for BIM Automation
hero.lead: InfoBIM IFC is a modular runtime environment for Engineering Automation designed to bridge the gap between static BIM data and dynamic AI agents.
hero.fordismo.title: Your Scripts, Evolved
hero.fordismo.body: Turns ad-hoc BIM scripts into a repeatable production line of capabilities, so agents can reuse them safely across projects.
hero.taylorismo.title: Two Approaches
hero.taylorismo.body: Whether you are a human or an AI Agent planning a workflow, InfoBIM IFC provides the standard.
hero.clt.title: Stable Runtime
hero.clt.body: You don't need to manually configure environments with IfcOpenShell or manage Python library conflicts.
hero.quote: “Engineers focus on what matters; the runtime handles the rest.”
discovery.title: Interface for Agents: The Discovery Protocol
discovery.lead1: InfoBIM is 'Agent-Friendly'. It provides a machine-readable catalog of tools, enabling LLMs to understand available functions and their parameters without hallucinations.
discovery.lead2: By wrapping codes in metadata, schema, and documentation, logic becomes discoverable and self-descriptive.
discovery.quote: Key Insight: Agents do not 'guess' geometry; they call Capabilities that return precise results.
challenge.week1.title: AI Agent
challenge.week4.title: AI Agent (Selecting Tool)
clash.title: Deep Dive: Clash Detection & Compliance
clash.subtitle: Automated logic checking.
clash.bullets.idsRules.label: IDS-native rules:
clash.bullets.idsRules.body: Supports buildingSMART IDS files to encode requirements as machine-readable contracts.
clash.bullets.propertyChecks.label: Property-first checks:
clash.bullets.propertyChecks.body: Software-agnostic checks using property sets, with official or custom schemas.
clash.bullets.alwaysOn.label: Always-on engine:
clash.bullets.alwaysOn.body: The engine runs continuously, applying complex engineering standards the same way every time.
clash.bullets.engineerInCommand.label: Engineer in command:
clash.bullets.engineerInCommand.body: The final decision stays with the engineer, who can override or refine any result.
reports.overview.title: Automatic Descriptive Reports
reports.overview.body: InfoBIM turns descriptive reports into code. It exposes a machine-readable catalog of Capabilities that generate Markdown sections for each chapter of the technical narrative - directly from IFC models.
reports.overview.lead: Instead of hand-writing memorials, agents orchestrate Capabilities for project identification, floor summaries, spaces, and systems. Each Capability has metadata, schema, and documentation, so the report structure is discoverable, traceable, and repeatable.
reports.overview.quote: Agents do not write memorials from scratch; they call Capabilities that output verifiable Markdown blocks.
reports.cards.faster.title: Faster memorials
reports.cards.faster.body: Project identification and context are generated directly from IFCs as ready‑to‑use Markdown sections.
reports.cards.traceable.title: Traceable narrative
reports.cards.traceable.body: Each paragraph in the descriptive report is backed by Capabilities, so every statement can be traced back to model data.
reports.cards.capabilityDriven.title: Capability-driven reports
reports.cards.capabilityDriven.body: Memorial sections are generated by Capabilities (project identification, floors, spaces, systems) in consistent Markdown ready for human review.
reports.cards.reusableKnowledge.title: Reusable knowledge
reports.cards.reusableKnowledge.body: Narrative logic lives in Capabilities and Usecases, not in one person’s head, so new projects reuse the same automated memorial pipeline.
pricing.title: InfoBIM is FREE and Open-Source
pricing.subtitle: Use InfoBIM IFC at no cost: run it self-hosted or work with an implementor who manages infrastructure, DevOps and long-term support.
pricing.image.badge.body: Run InfoBIM on your own infrastructure: clone the GitHub repository, configure your projects and start the services in your environment.
pricing.image.implementor.title: IMPLEMENTOR SUPPORT
pricing.image.implementor.body: Work with a specialized implementor who handles installation, infrastructure and ongoing DevOps support for your InfoBIM deployment.
pricing.image.body: Example of a visual inconsistency between calculation and drawing that InfoBIM helps surface and track for engineers and agents.
community.title: Join the InfoBIM Community
community.subtitle: InfoBIM is designed for collaboration. Join the InfoBIM Community to share capabilities, experiments or integrate the engine in real-world IFC automation recipes.
community.card1.title: Github
community.card1.body: Source code, issues and capability examples for InfoBIM. The foundational infrastructure for the next generation of BIM automation.
community.card2.title: Discord
community.card2.body: Real-time chat with other engineers to discuss pipelines, standards and automation.
community.card3.title: Blog
community.card3.body: Follow deep dives on IFC automation, capabilities and real projects in periodic articles.
footer.disclaimer: InfoBIM IFC is provided as open-source software. Validate results and configurations before using it in production environments.
footer.rights: All trademarks, product names and company names are the property of their respective owners.
footer.madeInBrazil: Proudly developed in Brazil 🇧🇷, so far.
